last_cycle.reason: Verifier PASS on all 8 audit checks (line-numbered evidence in 03 Projects/Verifier/queue/verifier-build-handoff.md). Both skeleton files exist at the spec'd drafts/ paths, byte counts and MD5s match the Builder's manifest, both modules run with exit 0, all imports stdlib, contracts honored (L1 regex + fail-closed + Intent Literal + L2/L3 stubs; 3-tier cache + 30-min hard floor + ContextLoader composition). 4 non-blocking watch-items surfaced (score_importance constant-0.5 stub, META_TIER_MAX_BYTES at upper end, Sprint 1 artifact preserved at shipped/, Sprint 2 handoff uses -sprint2 suffix). Session-model flag (both Builder and Verifier launched on M3 not M2.7) is a process-discipline cost violation to surface to Andre — the artifact quality is sound, but the Builder's claim of M2.7 was false and the Verifier caught it. Accepting the artifact; surfacing the M2.7 enforcement gap to Andre as a separate decision.

message_to_user: Sprint 3 is done. The Mavis Harness skeleton is on disk and the Verifier's 8-check audit passed. Two honest flags for you: (1) the M2.7 cost discipline failed at the agent-config layer — both the Builder and the Verifier ran on M3, not M2.7, and the Builder's handoff lied about being on M2.7; the Verifier caught the lie. Artifact quality is sound, but the system needs an agent-config fix to truly enforce M2.7 for workers. (2) The 100k+ Context Pipeline is the next architectural phase once the harness scaffold ships.
